cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 3

notes:
*i'm awarding a PERFECT score *ONLY* for the MP exectuable, as in the game and engine should be fine, so the singleplayer executable is clearly drm or something stupid stopping it starting AT ALL)

okay, in summary the biggest issues are that SINGLE player executable does NOT start. that means the co-op is not accessable...
the game can't be searched on protonDB (i had to enter another game's page then enter the 115300 appid to direct here manually)
barring this, the game seems to operate perfectly ONCE you turn "sync every frame on". 
no esync toggle, or d3d trickery.
that single player though is a big issue.
i've not yet TESTED the game in any serious respect though. i've simply started private matches, ran around for a minute or two, destroyed a few cars/chickens.
then exited and tampered with proton settings. 
eync seems to have MINOR performance gains on usb2.0 external Hdd loading. barring that. nothing.
------------------
remember, my system is running a "EXPERIMENTAL" feature for vulkan on the r9 270x chip.  so any success i have, MAY indicate regressions in newer drivers. (but i doubt it) (i'm also a linux ametuer, mostly windows experience).
title: Call of Duty®: Modern Warfare® 3

notes:
*UPDATE only, i'm not reporting the SP working yet. but i am reporting on the attempts to make it work. the MP has *NOT* been touched since my previous report.
since i'm posting a update, i've lowered the badge to indicate a lack of progress, but not bork the protondb rating for the MP section at least...
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
in summary. i've used winetricks to force a emulated desktop of 1600x900, tried changing every setting i could reach inside there. 
i've tried installing VARIOUS applications/extensions via winetricks. the entire directx catalog is currently installed.
interestingly during my messing around, I broke rundll32 (vcruntime 2015 i think). but something installed much later fixed it. beyond that. i've installed anything i can reasonibly suspect or even less likely suspect of being the culprit.
it's got to be something stupid, since the MP portion works fine.
